title: Bridge Essay: Hierarchical predictive coding in distributed pain circuits.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dependent Arising** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Dependent arising — predictive processing, free energy principle, active inference as causal cognition.
**Phase context:** Predictive processing — free energy principle, active inference, Bayesian brain.

**Hierarchical predictive coding in distributed pain circuits.** — Front Neural Circuits (2023). DOI: 10.3389/fncir.2023.1073537. PMID: 36937818. Predictive coding is a computational theory on describing how the brain perceives and acts, which has been widely adopted in sensory processing and motor control. Nociceptive and pain processing involves a large and distributed network of circuits. However, it is still unknown whether this distributed network is completely decentralized or requires networkwide coordination.
